name: Presence Patrols

backfire.note: Foreign boots past the welcome threshold generate antibodies (Kilcullen). Casualty drip handled via casualty_risk → Domestic.

notes: Suppresses Strength and raises insurgent Visibility while active.

backfire.note: Cheap attrition, but partner-force abuses carry your flag — Local and International pay for it.
Fast and no body bags — until the collateral roll: a crater, a martyrdom narrative, a recruitment spike via Grievance.

Cheap force multiplier — and every cycle plants a coup seed: trained officers raise junta odds on the collapse roll (several Sahel coup leaders were Western-trained). Arms leakage to the graph lands v0.4.
Wells, roads, schools — slow Local gains; in contested regions the shadow government taxes the project and the insurgency profits from your aid (negative attrit = strength gain).

name: Amnesty & Reintegration

backfire.note: Drains Strength via Defection and costs you 'soft on terror' at home up front; if security lapses, reintegrated fighters return to the fight.

sources: RAND, How Insurgencies End — negotiated drawdowns

backfire.note: The only counter to deceptive calm — it reveals Entrenchment truth. A blown network burns the people who trusted you.
A cheap legitimacy umbrella that is slow and vetoable — a stalled resolution reads as weakness at home and abroad.

name: Fund Independent Research

backfire.note: Endow think tanks and academics to document a regime — slow, durable Exposure that raises the price of its no-strings patron. But state money is an attack surface: 'foreign puppet' discrediting can blunt the very credibility you bought (the OCCRP funding paradox).

notes: The empirical backbone of the §20 Exposure system: research that survives denial.
sources: Global Witness / The Sentry → Magnitsky designation pipeline

name: Support Exile Media

backfire.note: Exile journalists move faster than think tanks — but the regime reaches their families and local fixers (transnational repression, Pegasus). The human cost is real, and the game does not flinch from it.

notes: Faster Exposure, carried by people the regime can hurt (§20.4).

name: Targeted Designations

backfire.note: Spend accumulated Exposure to sanction regime figures: strips patron influence and rolls back a bloc's consolidation. Needs the evidence first — a thin case (low Exposure) is dismissed and costs you credibility. The regime retaliates by expelling your people and NGOs.

notes: Converts Exposure into pressure — the documented research→designation chain (§20.4).
sources: Human Rights First — Global Magnitsky dossiers

name: Negotiate a Settlement

backfire.note: The Rebel Inc endgame: where a faction is stalemated — too strong to crush, not strong enough to win — talks can settle it, draining its Forces and shadow control. Costs you 'soft on terror' at home, earns International respect, and is often the only clean exit. Spoilers may attack the table to break it.

notes: The negotiation endgame (§7 diplomatic). Only bites at genuine stalemate.
sources: RAND, How Insurgencies End — negotiated settlements

backfire.note: Tier 1 of the Emergency Powers track: real, immediate intel power everywhere — and the Authoritarian Drift is permanent either way. The track must be tempting. That is the point.
